name: FreeFlyer
description: A commercial astrodynamics software platform for mission design, orbit determination, and spacecraft operations. Used by major satellite operators for high-fidelity orbit analysis, conjunction assessment, and autonomous manoeuvre planning.
whyUseIt: FreeFlyer excels at operational astrodynamics — the day-to-day orbit management that keeps satellites safe and in the right place. Its scripting language makes it ideal for automating repetitive orbital analyses and building custom mission planning workflows.

vocabulary.definition: Evaluating the probability of collision between your satellite and another object — critical for space safety
Calculating the optimal thrust direction and duration to move a satellite to a new orbit while minimising propellant use

beginnerMistakes: Not accounting for atmospheric drag uncertainty in low-orbit predictions
Using insufficient force models for high-precision orbit determination
Ignoring the effect of solar activity on atmospheric drag
